turns.content: Hey, I'm struggling with the new hire JSON spec. I've read about skip_serializing_if and Option in serde docs, but when my code compiles, the field still shows up as null in my schema instead of being omitted entirely.

That's a classic misunderstanding between Rust's Option type and JSON representation. You're likely using skip_serializing_if incorrectly or missing that it only skips if the condition is true. When you have an Option<T>, the field exists in the struct definition, so serde will serialize it unless you explicitly tell it to skip based on whether the Option contains Some or None.

Oh right! So I thought if I just mark a field as Optional in my macro config, that automatically handles skipping nulls. But I'm getting errors about missing derive attributes because I didn't use the proper skip logic.

Exactly. The `#[serde(skip)]` attribute is different from `skip_serializing_if`. You need to implement a closure that returns true when you want to skip, like `skip_serializing_if(|v| v.is_none())`. This ensures the field only appears in JSON if it has Some value, otherwise it's completely absent. For truly optional fields where absence matters more than null presence, serde_with provides double-option handling which combines both Option and Null semantics.
